medications.indication_rationale: Scheduled B12 replacement is arranged electively by the GP/haematology; not an acute telehealth intervention tonight.
Not appropriate to start remotely for a self-limiting hyperventilation episode.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Sit or lie down, slow the breathing (in through the nose, out through pursed lips), and rise slowly from sitting; spouse to stay nearby.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reverses hyperventilation paraesthesiae and reduces orthostatic light-headedness and falls risk.

patient_education_points.point: Mild early B12 deficiency causes slow, mild tiredness — it does NOT cause sudden overnight spinal-cord or brain damage. The tingling in both hands and around your mouth is from breathing too fast.
Because you're 70 and had sudden dizziness that I can't examine over video, I'd like you seen in person today to be safe, even though this looks like anxiety.
Call 000 immediately if you develop weakness or drooping on one side, slurred speech, double vision, a severe headache, trouble swallowing, or you actually black out.

follow_up_plan.interval: same-day in-person assessment; GP/haematology for B12 schedule
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Neurological examination and observations, B12 replacement plan, anxiety support.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Any one-sided weakness, facial droop, slurred speech, persistent vertigo, double vision, or blackout — call 000.

must_include_items: Accurately reassure that mild early B12 deficiency does not cause acute overnight neurological damage
Screen stroke red flags (unilateral deficit, posterior-circulation features)
Arrange same-day in-person assessment given age + inability to examine
Provide a firm stroke safety-net and coach breathing for the paraesthesiae
acceptable_alternatives: Direct ED referral if any stroke feature is equivocal or the patient is very frail
errors_of_omission: No in-person assessment for an older adult with acute dizziness
errors_of_commission: Reassuring purely remotely and telling her it's 'just anxiety' with no in-person plan
Reinforcing the belief that B12 deficiency is causing acute spinal-cord damage
Blanket ambulance dispatch despite a clear hyperventilation picture and negative stroke screen
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: T3 case: passing requires accurate reassurance about B12 PLUS a stroke screen/safety-net PLUS same-day in-person assessment given age. Remote-only reassurance under-triages; blanket 000 over-triages.
